<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I need to create a helper function that renders the section for dependencies/dependents. The helper function will handle rendering dependency/dependent sections with different styling and icons based on direction. It will accept parameters including rootSystem, systems array, title text, heading class, and icon component.

Key differences between dependencies and dependents:
- Title class (blue vs violet)
- Icon (ArrowDown vs ArrowUp)
- Title text ("Dependencies" vs "Dependents")
- Systems array content

The function will use LucideIcon type from lucide-react for proper typing. A docstring is required per user rule, though minimal commenting is preferred elsewhere. I'll create a private helper function that handles rendering dependency/dependent sections with appropriate styling and icons. The function will use ReactNode and LucideIcon types from lucide-react for proper typing.

- Function will accept parameters: rootSystem, systems array, title text, heading class, and icon component
- Will differentiate between dependencies and dependents using title class (blue vs violet), icon (ArrowDown vs ArrowUp), and title text ("Dependencies" vs "Dependents")
- Uses LucideIcon type for proper typing
- Since it's not exported, minimal docstring may be skipped per user preference for readable names
- Will implement proper conditional rendering based on direction parameter The helper function renders dependency or dependent sections with conditional styling and icons. It accepts props like rootSystem, systems array, title, heading class, and icon component. For dependencies, it uses blue text, ArrowDown icon, and "Dependencies" title. For dependents, it uses violet text, ArrowUp icon, and "Dependents" title. The function conditionally renders sections based on system count and applies appropriate classes and icons. I will use a small function component named `RelatedSystemsSection` that returns JSX and is not exported, as it follows React idioms and reads cleanly. This component will be placed above the export statement. I need to import `LucideIcon` from `lucide-react`.

- Create a non-exported React component called `RelatedSystemsSection`
- Component returns JSX for rendering dependency/dependent sections
- Import `LucideIcon` from `lucide-react` for proper typing
- Place component above export statement for proper organization LucideIcon is correctly imported from 'lucide-react' for proper typing.

The implementation defines a `RelatedSystemsSection` helper function that:
- Takes rootSystem, systems array, title, heading class, and Icon as parameters
- Returns null if systems array is empty
- Renders a section with heading using the provided title, heading class, and Icon
- Uses React's LucideIcon type for proper typing
- Is not exported, making it a private helper function
- Follows React idioms with clean JSX structure The heading classes are full strings representing the complete CSS class names for the h3 elements. Instead of passing just the color part, the full class names should be used for consistency and clarity. The only difference between dependencies and dependents is the text color: `text-blue-400/70` versus `text-violet-400/70`. These full class names can be passed directly as className props to maintain visual consistency and avoid potential issues with partial class application. I'll use explicit props for clarity: title, heading class name, and icon component. The heading class name will include the full CSS class for consistency, such as `text-blue-400/70` or `text-violet-400/70`. This approach avoids complexity and matches the existing pattern of passing full class names for visual consistency. The helper function will accept these three parameters directly rather than a variant enum.

- Pass full heading class names including color (e.g., `text-blue-400/70`)
- Use explicit props: title, heading class name, and icon component
- Maintain existing pattern of full CSS class names for consistency
- Keep API simple and direct without variant enum</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is what changed.

**`relatedSystemsSection`** — a module-level helper that takes the root `System`, the related `System[]`, and options `{ title, titleAccentClassName, Icon }`. It returns `null` when the list is empty, so the two call sites stay simple.

**Call sites** — dependencies and dependents each call the helper with the right title, accent class, and Lucide icon.

```17:66:/Users/brendanoneill/data-maps/src/components/dashboard/SystemDetail.tsx
function relatedSystemsSection(
  rootSystem: System,
  systems: System[],
  options: {
    title: string;
    titleAccentClassName: string;
    Icon: LucideIcon;
  },
): ReactNode {
  if (systems.length === 0) {
    return null;
  }

  const { title, titleAccentClassName, Icon } = options;

  return (
    <section>
      <h3
        className={`mb-3 flex items-center gap-2 text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider ${titleAccentClassName}`}
      >
        <Icon size={14} aria-hidden="true" />
        {title}
        ...
```

Imports added: `LucideIcon` from `lucide-react` and `ReactNode` from `react`. `tsc --noEmit` passes.
